meta.title: Triangle Opportunity Guide
meta.subtitle: Careers Without a Four-Year Degree
meta.subsubtitle: What Is Available, What It Pays, and How to Get There
meta.publisher: United Way of the Greater Triangle
meta.region: Triangle: Durham, Johnston, Orange, Wake counties

dataSources.criminalRecord: NC 2019 Freedom to Work Act framing

meta.refreshStrategy: Wage and projection fields refreshed monthly from BLS OEWS API (api.bls.gov) and Lightcast (paid). Training program details verified quarterly against institutional websites. Employer lists and qualitative content reviewed twice yearly.

sectors.name: Healthcare
sectors.tagline: The Triangle's fastest-growing sector — strongest sub-BA pathways in the region

headlineStats.label: RN avg age — retirements drive demand
Job seekers per open position in 2023

sectors.summary: Largest sector. Duke Health, UNC Health, WakeMed dominate. CNA to RN to NP pathway. Associate degree opens most doors.

sectors.name: Life Sciences Manufacturing
sectors.tagline: Triangle biotech and pharma boom — $14.7B investment creating thousands of production jobs
sectors.summary: Triangle is a national biotech hub. Novo Nordisk, Pfizer, GSK expanding. NC BioNetwork offers free training.

sectors.name: Skilled Trades
sectors.tagline: 29% of all regional job openings — Triangle building boom driving unprecedented demand

headlineStats.label: IBEW pays from day one, no debt

sectors.summary: Triangle building boom driving unprecedented demand. IBEW apprenticeship pays from day one. Many employers train on the job.

sectors.name: IT and Technology
sectors.tagline: RTP tech concentration — sub-BA pathways in networking and cybersecurity are strong
sectors.summary: Sub-BA roles strongest in networking and cybersecurity. Certifications often matter more than degrees. Remote/hybrid widely available.

sectors.name: Transportation and Logistics
sectors.tagline: High volume of openings year-round — e-commerce and Triangle growth driving demand
sectors.summary: CDL takes 6-8 weeks. Large carriers sponsor training. Short-haul more secure than long-haul from automation.

sectors.name: Public Sector
sectors.tagline: Stable employment across all four counties — best benefits package in the region

headlineStats.label: Benefits in region: pension + State Health Plan
Most agencies fund their own training
Sub-BA roles in local government Triangle

sectors.summary: State Health Plan + pension. 4 high-opportunity pathways (P2G 2026): Utilities, Maintenance, Social Work, Electric Line Workers.

occupations.title: Medical Assistant
occupations.settings: Physician practices, clinics, specialty offices, Triangle-wide
occupations.summary: Support physicians with patient intake, vitals, injections, and scheduling in outpatient clinics.

byTheNumbers.label: Clear path to LPN and RN

quickFacts: One of the few M-F schedules in clinical healthcare
No licensure required — faster entry path
Best stepping stone into LPN and RN programs
AI handles admin tasks; clinical work stays human
whatYoullDo: Support physicians with patient intake, vitals, injections, and scheduling.
Work in physician offices and outpatient clinics — typically M-F schedule.
500+ private practices in Durham and Wake County hiring regularly.
Bridge role — MA experience strengthens LPN and RN program applications.

jobQuality.benefits: Variable by practice size — larger clinics better; small practices vary
jobQuality.schedule: M-F daytime typical — no shift work in most roles; rare in clinical healthcare
jobQuality.advancement: MA to LPN to RN — clear career ladder; clinical experience valued
jobQuality.automation: AI handles admin tasks — clinical tasks remain human-performed

occupations.title: Medical Records Specialist
occupations.settings: Health systems, clinics, remote-friendly, Triangle-wide

tags.automationRisk: HIGHER RISK — SPECIALIZE TO STAY AHEAD

occupations.summary: Organize, code, and manage patient health records in EHR systems.
quickFacts: Only healthcare role with true remote options
No licensure required — faster entry
Specialized coding (CCS) most resistant to automation
AI handles basic data entry; coding skills valued
whatYoullDo: Organize, code, and manage patient health information in EHR systems.
Medical coding (ICD-10, CPT) is most resistant to automation — and best paid.
Remote-friendly: many Triangle systems offer hybrid and fully remote roles.
No licensure required — background check policy set by employer.

trainingPrograms.name: Durham Technical CC — Health Info Technology
trainingPrograms.highlight: Prepares for CCS and RHIT creds

trainingPrograms.name: Wake Technical CC — Medical Office Admin
IQVIA and Duke Health — HIM Apprenticeships

jobQuality.benefits: Strong at large systems — Duke, UNC, WakeMed full benefits packages
jobQuality.schedule: M-F typical — hybrid and remote widely available in Triangle
jobQuality.advancement: Coder to HIM Mgr — CCS raises wages sharply; clear ladder to $50+/hr
jobQuality.automation: Data entry at risk — specialized coding is secure; ICD-10/CPT skills valued

occupations.title: Licensed Practical Nurse
occupations.settings: Hospitals, long-term care, clinics, Durham and Raleigh MSAs
occupations.summary: Provide direct patient care under RN supervision in hospitals, long-term care, and clinics.

byTheNumbers.label: Most large employers offer LPN-to-RN path

quickFacts: Fastest licensed nursing pathway
Large systems sponsor LPN-to-RN bridge programs
Strong demand in long-term care and home health
CNA experience is a strong preparation
whatYoullDo: Provide direct patient care under RN supervision in every care setting.
Administer medications, monitor patients, perform procedures, document care.
LPN is the fastest path to licensed nursing — and a proven on-ramp to RN.
Strong demand in long-term care, home health, and physician offices.

jobQuality.benefits: Strong at large systems — long-term care benefits vary; ask before accepting
jobQuality.schedule: Shift work common — some M-F clinic roles in physician offices
jobQuality.advancement: LPN to RN bridge — most large employers sponsor; clear path to $40+/hr
jobQuality.demands: Direct patient care — physically demanding; high-volume care environments

occupations.title: Physical Therapist Assistant
occupations.settings: Outpatient clinics, hospitals, rehab facilities, Triangle-wide
occupations.summary: Help patients recover from injury and surgery through guided exercise and therapy.

byTheNumbers.label: Most outpatient roles; no shift work needed

quickFacts: Best chance of M-F schedule in clinical healthcare
EmergeOrtho is the largest regional employer
Ladder to full PT requires bachelor + masters
Hands-on work with low automation exposure
whatYoullDo: Help patients recover from injury and surgery through therapeutic exercise.
Triangle's aging population and orthopedic surgery volume drive strong demand.
Most outpatient clinic roles offer M-F schedules — rare in clinical healthcare.
EmergeOrtho, Duke Orthopedics, and 100+ outpatient clinics actively hiring.

jobQuality.benefits: Good at large clinics — outpatient practices vary; ask about health/401k
jobQuality.schedule: M-F in outpatient — hospital roles have shifts; outpatient is typical M-F
jobQuality.advancement: PTA to PT path — requires bachelor + masters; high ceiling if you continue
jobQuality.demands: Physically active — rewarding patient outcomes; lower stress than ICU

occupations.title: Radiologic Technologist
occupations.settings: Hospitals, imaging centers, specialty practices, Triangle-wide

tags.automationRisk: MEDIUM RISK — ROLE EVOLVING WITH AI

occupations.summary: Perform diagnostic imaging including X-ray, CT, and MRI for patients and physicians.

byTheNumbers.label: Durham MSA imaging concentration vs national

quickFacts: Durham has 3x national concentration of imaging roles
AI reads scans; positioning and equipment stay human
CT/MRI specialization adds $5-10/hr
On-call availability increases opportunities
whatYoullDo: Perform X-rays, CT, MRI, and diagnostic imaging for patients and physicians.
Durham MSA has 3.12x the national concentration of imaging professionals.
AI assists image reading but patient positioning and equipment stay human.
CT, MRI, or mammography specialization adds $5-10/hr above base pay.

trainingPrograms.name: UNC Health / Duke — Clinical Partnerships
trainingPrograms.highlight: Both systems partner w/ CC programs

jobQuality.benefits: Excellent at large systems — strong health and 401k; Duke, UNC, WakeMed
jobQuality.schedule: Mix of M-F and shifts — some on-call required; hospital includes nights
jobQuality.advancement: RT to CT/MRI — specialization adds $5-10/hr; management track avail.
jobQuality.automation: AI reads images — patient care stays essential; role evolves not disappears

occupations.title: Respiratory Therapist
occupations.settings: Hospitals, ICU, pulmonary clinics, Durham and Raleigh MSAs
occupations.summary: Treat patients with breathing disorders including asthma, COPD, COVID, and respiratory failure.
quickFacts: COVID elevated RT visibility and demand
Aging population ensures sustained demand
RRT credential (NBRC) raises wages $5-8/hr
Critical care specialization valued
whatYoullDo: Treat patients with asthma, COPD, COVID, and respiratory failure.
Operate ventilators, perform breathing tests, educate patients on lung health.
Work in ICU, emergency, neonatal, and pulmonary rehab settings.
Aging population and respiratory disease trends driving sustained demand.

trainingPrograms.name: RRT Credential (NBRC) — Post-ADN
trainingPrograms.highlight: Raises wages $5-8/hr; can complete while working

jobQuality.benefits: Excellent at large systems — full benefits; shift differentials apply
jobQuality.schedule: 12-hr hospital shifts — some M-F clinic options; hospital includes nights
jobQuality.advancement: RT to Specialist — RRT credential raises wages; management track available
jobQuality.demands: High-stakes ICU work — emotionally demanding; rewarding patient outcomes

occupations.title: Registered Nurse
occupations.settings: Hospitals, clinics, home health, Durham and Raleigh MSAs
occupations.summary: Assess patients, administer medications, and coordinate care across hospitals, clinics, and home health settings.

byTheNumbers.label: Avg US RN age — retirements drive openings
Job seekers per open position in 2023

quickFacts: Retirements (not new growth) drive most RN openings
Both Durham and Raleigh MSAs hiring at scale
Large systems sponsor LPN-to-RN bridge programs
CNA or Medical Assistant is the standard entry on-ramp
whatYoullDo: Assess patients, administer medications, and coordinate care with physicians.
Duke Health, UNC Health, and WakeMed hire RNs at scale across the Triangle.
Settings: med-surg, ICU, emergency, pediatric, outpatient, and home health.
Shift differentials for nights and weekends add $3-8/hr above median.

trainingPrograms.name: Durham Technical CC — Associate Degree Nursing
Wake Technical CC — ADN and LPN Programs

jobQuality.benefits: Excellent at large systems — health, 401k, tuition assist; smaller clinics vary
jobQuality.schedule: 3x12-hr hospital shifts — M-F outpatient avail; shift diff $3-8/hr
jobQuality.advancement: RN to Charge to NP — employers sponsor education; clear ladder to $62+/hr
jobQuality.demands: High-stakes environments — physically and emotionally intense; shift work required

occupations.title: Certified Nursing Assistant
occupations.settings: Hospitals, long-term care, home health, hospice — Triangle-wide
occupations.summary: Provide direct patient care under nursing supervision — bathing, feeding, vitals, mobility help. Fastest legal entry into clinical healthcare in NC and the most common springboard to LPN and RN.

byTheNumbers.label: Open CNA postings across the Triangle
Most common springboard to LPN and RN

quickFacts: Fastest legal entry into clinical healthcare in NC
Strong CNA → LPN → RN ladder; most NC nursing schools require or prefer CNA experience
Shift work is the norm; many facilities offer weekend or night differentials
High turnover means employers are constantly hiring — easier to land first job
whatYoullDo: Help patients with bathing, feeding, dressing, and mobility.
Take vital signs and document care in the EHR.
Reposition patients to prevent pressure injuries; assist with transfers.
Communicate changes in patient condition to the nursing team.

trainingPrograms.name: Wake Tech — Nurse Aide I
trainingPrograms.highlight: Approved by NC Board of Nursing

jobQuality.schedule: Shifts (8-12 hr) — days, evenings, nights, weekends typical at 24/7 facilities
jobQuality.benefits: Varies widely — large hospital systems include health/retirement/paid leave; smaller LTC and home-health agencies are more variable
jobQuality.advancement: CNA II, charge CNA, LPN bridge, RN ADN bridge — clear vertical ladder
jobQuality.automation: Low — hands-on personal care doesn't automate

occupations.title: Community Health Worker
occupations.settings: Community health centers, public health departments, nonprofit clinics, hospital outreach teams — Triangle-wide
occupations.summary: Connect underserved patients to healthcare, social services, transportation, and education. Often hired from the community served. Strong M-F roles at federally qualified health centers, public health departments, and hospital outreach teams.

byTheNumbers.label: Medicaid now covers CHW services in NC
Common path to social work or nursing

topEmployers.name: Durham County Department of Public Health

quickFacts: Spanish bilingual hires earn premium and have many more openings
Most CHW roles are M-F daytime — rare in clinical healthcare
NC Medicaid now reimburses CHW services, fueling employer demand
Common bridge into social work, nursing, or public health admin
whatYoullDo: Connect patients to clinical, social, and transportation resources.
Conduct home or community-based check-ins; help with appointment navigation.
Provide basic health education on chronic disease management.
Document encounters in the EHR or population-health platforms.

trainingPrograms.name: Durham Tech — CHW Certificate
trainingPrograms.highlight: Designed with Lincoln CHC and Duke

jobQuality.schedule: M-F daytime typical; some evening community events
jobQuality.benefits: Strong at FQHCs and public health departments — federal/state coverage, retirement, paid leave
jobQuality.advancement: Lead CHW, CHW supervisor, program coordinator, social worker (with degree)
jobQuality.automation: Low — community trust and human connection don't automate

occupations.title: Clinical Research Coordinator
occupations.settings: Academic medical centers, contract research orgs (CROs), pharma sites — Duke, UNC, WakeMed, and Triangle CROs
occupations.summary: Coordinate clinical trials — patient enrollment, regulatory paperwork, data entry, sample collection. The Triangle is a national hub for trials. Titles, entry levels, and degree requirements vary widely between Duke, UNC, hospital systems, and CROs.

variability.titles: Titles vary widely: Clinical Research Coordinator, Clinical Research Assistant, CRC I/II, Research Specialist, Study Coordinator. The same work can sit at different levels at different employers.
variability.requirements: Minimum qualifications differ across institutions. Some employers (Duke, UNC for senior roles) require a 4-year degree. Others — many CROs and entry-level academic positions — accept a 2-year degree plus relevant experience, a CCRP/SoCRA certification, or strong healthcare background (MA, CNA, lab tech).
variability.advancement: One employer's ladder may not match another's. CRO ladders (CRA → Sr CRA → Project Manager) differ from academic ladders (CRC → Sr CRC → Research Manager).

byTheNumbers.label: Most roles, though some patient visits flex
Common entry from MA, lab tech, CNA experience

topEmployers.name: Duke Office of Clinical Research
topEmployers.note: Largest academic trials sponsor in the Triangle

quickFacts: Titles and degree requirements vary — read each job posting closely
Some employers waive degree requirements with CCRP or SoCRA certification + healthcare experience
Strong path for MAs, CNAs, and lab techs looking for M-F desk-based work with patient contact
Triangle's CRO and academic medical concentration means more openings here than most US metros
whatYoullDo: Screen and enroll patients into clinical trials per the protocol.
Maintain regulatory binders, IRB submissions, and documentation.
Collect samples, vital signs, and case-report-form data.
Coordinate sponsor monitor visits and respond to data queries.

trainingPrograms.name: Durham Tech — Clinical Trials Research Associate
Wake Tech — Clinical Research Coordinator certificate
SoCRA CCRP credential
trainingPrograms.highlight: Widely recognized — many employers waive degree with this

jobQuality.schedule: M-F daytime typical at academic medical centers; some patient visits flex into evenings
jobQuality.benefits: Strong at Duke / UNC / WakeMed and large CROs — comprehensive health, retirement, paid leave
jobQuality.advancement: Varies by employer — CROs use CRA/Sr CRA/PM ladders; academic centers use CRC I/II/Sr ladders
jobQuality.automation: Low — regulated trial conduct requires human oversight

occupations.title: Quality Control Inspector

occupations.summary: Inspect products, review documentation, and ensure pharmaceutical quality standards.

byTheNumbers.label: Demand at every pharma site in region
Attention to detail and documentation key

quickFacts: Every pharma manufacturing facility needs QC
Accuracy and documentation drive advancement
ASQ Quality Inspector cert accelerates career
QA Manager ($48+/hr) is the natural ceiling
whatYoullDo: Inspect products and review documentation to ensure FDA and company quality standards are met.
Every pharmaceutical manufacturing facility requires QC inspectors — steady demand.
Attention to detail and accurate documentation are more important than formal credentials.
Clear advancement path from Inspector to QC Lead to QA Manager.

trainingPrograms.name: Employer OJT — Novo Nordisk/Pfizer/Grifols
trainingPrograms.highlight: Most pharma employers train QC internally

jobQuality.benefits: Strong at large pharma — full benefits at Novo Nordisk, Pfizer, GSK, Catalent
jobQuality.schedule: Rotating shifts — 12-hr rotating; shift diff adds meaningful income
jobQuality.advancement: Inspector to QA — QA cert accelerates advancement; clear internal ladder
jobQuality.demands: Detail and docs — FDA audits; accurate records are non-negotiable

occupations.title: CDL Truck Driver

tags.criminalRecord: RECORD: CDL HAS FEDERAL RESTRICTIONS — CHECK FMCSA

occupations.summary: Transport freight and goods across local, regional, and national routes.

byTheNumbers.label: CDL cert — one of fastest trade creds
Volume driven by turnover and growth

quickFacts: CDL is one of the fastest trade credentials
Short-haul and local delivery more automation-secure
Large carriers (Amazon, FedEx) sponsor CDL training
Federal CDL restrictions apply to records
whatYoullDo: Transport goods across local, regional, and national routes for major carriers.
High volume of openings driven by turnover and an aging driver workforce.
CDL certificate takes only 6-8 weeks — one of the fastest trade credentials.
Short-haul and local delivery roles are more secure from automation.

jobQuality.benefits: Variable — Amazon, FedEx, UPS good; smaller carriers vary widely
jobQuality.schedule: Irregular hours — local delivery predictable; long-haul time away
jobQuality.advancement: Driver to Manager — long path; dispatcher and logistics adjacent step
jobQuality.automation: Long-term risk — short-haul more secure; 10-15 yr horizon

occupations.title: Transportation / Logistics Manager

CDL entry to management in 5-10 years

CDL is often the entry point to eventual management
E-commerce growth sustains strong demand
whatYoullDo: Oversee transportation and logistics operations at distribution centers and carriers.
Manage driver teams, schedules, compliance, and customer expectations.
Experience pathway: no degree required — advancement from dispatcher or CDL roles.
E-commerce expansion and Triangle population growth sustaining demand.

trainingPrograms.name: Wake Technical CC — Logistics and SCM

jobQuality.automation: Decision-based work — judgment and team leadership stay human

resources.name: NCWorks Career Centers
resources.description: Free career coaching, job search, and WIOA funding eligibility. Walk-in or appointment.

resources.name: Durham Technical Community College
resources.description: Healthcare, IT, manufacturing, and trades programs. Financial aid and WIOA eligible.

resources.name: Wake Technical Community College
resources.description: Largest CC in NC. Multiple campuses across Wake County. Full range of healthcare, IT, and trades.

resources.name: NC BioNetwork
resources.description: Free and low-cost biomanufacturing and pharmaceutical production training. Best free entry point into pharma.

resources.name: IBEW Local 553 (Electricians)
resources.description: Paid electrician apprenticeship. Earn from day one, no tuition debt, full benefits from day one.

resources.name: NC Justice Center / Legal Aid of NC
resources.description: NC's 2019 Freedom to Work Act protects most applicants from automatic license denial. Free legal aid.

resources.name: WIOA (Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act)
resources.description: Can cover tuition, fees, books, and sometimes transportation for qualifying adults. Ask NCWorks.

resources.name: United Way of the Greater Triangle
resources.description: Future of Work initiative: building aligned workforce pathways across Durham, Johnston, Orange, and Wake counties.

userGoals.label: I need a stable job within 6 months
userGoals.description: Show me roles that accept HS diploma or short certificates and pay $22+/hr.

userGoals.label: I want to retrain for a new career
userGoals.description: Show me roles where 1-2 years of training opens a strong career ladder.

userGoals.label: I have a job and want to earn more
userGoals.description: Show me bridge programs and roles I can move up to from where I am now.

userGoals.label: I want the best benefits package
userGoals.description: Show me roles with pension, State Health Plan, or full union benefits.

userGoals.label: I'm just exploring options
userGoals.description: Show me everything — I'll narrow down as I learn.
